title: melaleuca pauperiflora subsp. fastigiata
intro: Melaleuca pauperiflora subsp. fastigiata, also known as a small shrub native to Western Australia, is a plant species in the myrtle family, Myrtaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its essential oil content. The health benefits of Melaleuca pauperiflora subsp. fastigiata include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation and kill bacteria, making it a useful remedy for skin conditions and infections. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of essential oils, tinctures, and creams, which can be used topically to harness its medicinal properties.
